Long Description: A rectangular gold plate engraved with “Holy to YHWH” and attached to a linen turban with blue cords. It was a sacred symbol of the priest’s consecration.  The tzitz was not a royal crown but was still called a nezer because it marked the high priest’s holiness. It was a gold forehead plate tied to a turban, paralleling Egyptian and Levantine ceremonial headpieces. Its inscription made it uniquely Israelite, emphasizing purity and consecration before God.
Keywords: tzitz, high priest, gold plate, inscription, Holy to YHWH, turban, priesthood, tabernacle
